page: Microsoft Azure M64bds_1_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8473C processor operating at 4.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1397.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.83 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 3072 GB of capacity, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth is a highlight, reaching 4519.69 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 216.95 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Compute performance is supported by advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This is reflected in a Geekbench multi-core score of 19691.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 66425.61. Single-threaded gzip compression performs at 32.92 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 344.01 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance achieves 70.71 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a Passmark database score of 28843.47.

While the server excels in memory-intensive tasks and prompt processing for large language models (achieving 26.47 tokens/sec on a 70B model), it exhibits a performance tradeoff in text generation for very large models, dropping to 2.58 tokens/sec on the 70B model due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its massive memory footprint, high-speed local storage, and strong database benchmark results, the M64bds_1_v3 is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy enterprise database workloads, data-intensive analytics, and memory-bound scientific simulations.
description: The Microsoft Azure M64bds_1_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz. It features 1397.0 GB of RAM, providing 21.83 GB of memory per core, alongside 3072 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers strong memory bandwidth, reaching 4519.69 GB/sec for cached reads, and robust database performance, as evidenced by 70.71 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated mathematical operations. While its CPU-based architecture handles prompt processing efficiently, it exhibits lower throughput for large-scale LLM text generation due to the absence of a GPU. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise databases, in-memory analytics, and large-scale data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64bds_1_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1397 GB of RAM, and 3072 GB of local storage, designed for high-performance database operations and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64bds_1_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1397 GB of RAM, and 3072 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform with local storage, engineered for demanding in-memory databases and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8473C processor
1397.0 GB of RAM providing 21.83 GB per core
3072 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory bandwidth of up to 4519.69 GB/sec for cached reads
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and heavy enterprise workloads
Tradeoff in large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU acceleration
